entries.zhRaw: 擔任臨時保母／照顧小孩

candidate.example: My backpack holds my spelling notebook.

candidate.usage: Use it for a grown-up drink made from beans.

candidate.usage: Use it when something has many colors.

candidate.usage: Use it when something feels good and easy.

candidate.example: The comic shows a hero learning words.

candidate.usage: Use it for people living or working together.

candidate.example: The toy company made a puzzle game.
candidate.usage: Use it for a business.

candidate.usage: Use it when something is finished.

candidate.example: The computer plays the spelling sound.
candidate.usage: Use it for an electronic machine for work or games.

candidate.example: The school concert begins at seven.
candidate.usage: Use it for a music performance.

candidate.example: Practice gives me confidence.
candidate.usage: Use it for believing you can do something.

candidate.example: I feel confident after spelling each word aloud.
candidate.usage: Use it when someone believes they can do well.

candidate.example: The two similar words can confuse me.
candidate.usage: Use it when something is hard to understand.

candidate.example: We congratulate the winner after the round.
candidate.usage: Use it when you tell someone you are happy for them.

candidate.example: Connect the letters to build the word.
candidate.usage: Use it when two things join together.

candidate.example: Consider the vowel sound before you answer.
candidate.usage: Use it when you think carefully.

candidate.usage: Use it when you build something.

candidate.usage: Use it when something has something inside.

candidate.usage: Use it for a competition.

candidate.example: Continue spelling even if the word is long.
candidate.usage: Use it when you keep going.

candidate.usage: Use it when people work together.

candidate.usage: Use it when something is right.

candidate.usage: Use it for the money needed to buy something.

candidate.usage: Use it for special clothes.

candidate.usage: Use it for the child of your aunt or uncle.

candidate.usage: Use it for a person who works with cattle.

candidate.usage: Use it for a wax coloring stick.

candidate.example: The crazy race has letters flying everywhere.
candidate.usage: Use it carefully for something wild or silly.

candidate.usage: Use it for a thick milk food.

candidate.example: Create a sentence with the new word.
candidate.usage: Use it when you make something new.

candidate.example: A creative student draws word monsters.
candidate.usage: Use it for someone who has new ideas.

candidate.usage: Use it for a small jumping insect or a sport.

candidate.usage: Use it for a large reptile with strong jaws.

candidate.example: Stop and look before using the crosswalk.
candidate.usage: Use it for the safe place to cross a street.

candidate.example: Cucumber slices are crunchy.
candidate.usage: Use it for a long green vegetable.

candidate.example: A curious learner asks why a word is spelled that way.
candidate.usage: Use it when someone wants to know more.

candidate.example: I do a daily spelling warm-up before class.
candidate.usage: Use it when something happens every day.

candidate.example: Milk and cheese are dairy foods.

candidate.example: The storm did not damage our classroom window.
candidate.usage: Use it when something gets hurt or broken.

candidate.example: We dance to a happy song in music class.
candidate.usage: Use it for moving your body with music.

candidate.example: The teacher told us to stay away from danger.
candidate.usage: Use it when something is not safe.

candidate.usage: Use it to describe something not safe.

candidate.usage: Use it for a girl child in a family.

candidate.usage: Use it for the very early morning.

candidate.usage: Use it for light from the sun.

candidate.example: The project deadline is Friday.
candidate.usage: Use it for the last time to finish work.

candidate.example: A deaf student may use sign language.
candidate.usage: Use it respectfully for someone who cannot hear.

entries.zhRaw: 正派的／體面的／還不錯的

candidate.example: I decide to practice ten words today.
candidate.usage: Use it when you choose one thing.

candidate.example: We decorate the board with paper stars.
candidate.usage: Use it when you make something look nice.

candidate.example: The soup smells delicious.
candidate.usage: Use it for food that tastes very good.

candidate.example: The dentist checks my teeth.
candidate.usage: Use it for a doctor who cares for teeth.

candidate.example: The toy department is on the second floor.
candidate.usage: Use it for one part of a big store or office.

candidate.example: Describe the monster with three clear words.
candidate.usage: Use it when you tell what something is like.

candidate.example: A camel can walk across the desert.
candidate.usage: Use it for a very dry place with sand.

candidate.example: We had fruit for dessert.
candidate.usage: Use it for a sweet food after a meal.

candidate.example: I check the dictionary for a new word.
candidate.usage: Use it for a book or app that explains words.

candidate.example: These two cards have different colors.
candidate.usage: Use it when things are not the same.

candidate.example: This spelling word is difficult, but I can try.
candidate.usage: Use it when something is hard to do.

candidate.example: We use a digital timer during the quiz.
candidate.usage: Use it for something that uses computer technology.

candidate.example: I study five words after dinner.
candidate.usage: Use it for the evening meal.

candidate.example: The dinosaur model is taller than my desk.
candidate.usage: Use it for an ancient animal.

candidate.example: We divide the letters into two teams.
candidate.usage: Use it when you split something into parts.

candidate.example: I felt dizzy after spinning around.
candidate.usage: Use it when your head feels like it is turning.

candidate.example: The doctor helps me when I am sick.
candidate.usage: Use it for a person who cares for health.

candidate.example: A dolphin can jump out of the water.
candidate.usage: Use it for a smart sea animal.

candidate.example: The dragon guards a shiny word badge.
candidate.usage: Use it for a make-believe creature in stories.

candidate.example: A dragonfly landed near the pond.
candidate.usage: Use it for an insect with long wings.

candidate.example: I keep my word cards in a drawer.
candidate.usage: Use it for a sliding box in a desk.

candidate.example: I dream about winning the spelling bee.
candidate.usage: Use it for ideas while sleeping or a big hope.

candidate.example: Please drink water after practice.
candidate.usage: Use it when liquid goes into your mouth.

candidate.example: The bus driver waved to our class.
candidate.usage: Use it for a person who drives.

candidate.example: Do not drop the letter tiles.
candidate.usage: Use it when something falls.

candidate.example: The duck swims beside the duckling.
candidate.usage: Use it for a water bird.

candidate.example: The yellow duckling follows its mother.
candidate.usage: Use it for a baby duck.

candidate.example: I ate one dumpling before the quiz.
candidate.usage: Use it for a small filled food.
